message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

review each file under "test" 
test/test_frexp.c:202:13: warning: 'test_exhaustive_small' defined but not used [-Wunused-function]
  202 | static void test_exhaustive_small(void)
      |             ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
test/test_frexp.c:188:13: warning: 'test_sweep' defined but not used [-Wunused-function]
  188 | static void test_sweep(void)
  
when run 'make bench-all'

content.text: The two functions are only called from the `#else` branch but aren't guarded by `#ifndef EN_PERF`. Fix: wrap them.
Same issue in `test_frexpf.c`. Fix both by wrapping the unused functions in `#ifndef EN_PERF`.

content.text: The warning only appeared for `test_frexp.c` and `test_frexpf.c` per the original report. Let me verify the other files handle this correctly:

input.description: Find test files missing the EN_PERF guard entirely

input.command: make bench-all 2>&1 | grep "warning:" | grep -v "note:" | sort -u | head -30
